title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 39 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: Sydney New Year's Eve
festivals.description: Iconic harbour-side celebrations featuring the famous fireworks display over Sydney Harbour Bridge and Opera House, attracting over a million spectators. A globally televised event marking the year's end with spectacular pyrotechnics and live entertainment.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrated across all of Australia marking the beginning of the new year with family gatherings, barbecues, and community events.

festivals.name: Lamington National Park Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the natural beauty and biodiversity of this mountain rainforest with guided walks, wildlife spotting, and environmental talks. A perfect introduction to Queensland's subtropical wilderness.

festivals.name: Australia Day
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrating Australian culture with fireworks, parades, concerts, and citizenship ceremonies held across the country.

festivals.name: Tawny Frogmouth Festival
festivals.description: A quirky regional celebration in the Dandenong Ranges featuring crafts, live music, and wildlife education focused on the local tawny frogmouth bird. A charming small-town festival with a conservation message.

festivals.name: Perth Summer Festival
festivals.description: A vibrant month-long celebration of Perth's culture featuring outdoor concerts, theatre, dance, and community events across multiple venues. Showcases local and international artists in the warm summer weather.

festivals.name: Tropfest
festivals.description: The world's largest short film festival held annually, showcasing hundreds of short films from emerging and established filmmakers. A major cultural event celebrating independent cinema.

festivals.name: Adelaide Festival
festivals.description: One of Australia's premier arts festivals featuring theatre, music, dance, visual arts, and literature from world-class performers and artists. A prestigious biennial celebration of the performing arts.

festivals.name: Moomba Festival
festivals.description: Melbourne's largest free community festival featuring live music, comedy, rides, fireworks, and parades along the Yarra River. A beloved family event celebrating Australian culture with entertainment and fun.

festivals.name: Royal Canberra Show
festivals.description: A traditional agricultural show featuring livestock competitions, rural crafts, rides, and entertainment celebrating rural life and farming heritage. A family-friendly event with produce competitions and showbag giveaways.

festivals.name: Melbourne Food and Wine Festival
festivals.description: One of Australia's largest food festivals celebrating culinary excellence with chef demonstrations, wine tastings, and gourmet experiences. A premier event for foodies and wine enthusiasts.

festivals.name: Wildflower Season
festivals.description: Spring transforms Western Australia's landscape into a carpet of colourful native wildflowers, with guided tours and festivals throughout the region. A natural spectacle unique to WA's Mediterranean climate.

festivals.name: ANZAC Day
festivals.description: National public holiday honouring fallen soldiers with dawn services, parades, and commemorative events held across Australia. A solemn day of remembrance for military service.

festivals.name: Baw Baw Brewing and Arts Festival
festivals.description: A regional festival celebrating craft brewing, local arts, and mountain culture in the Baw Baw region. Features live music, craft beer tastings, and artisan markets.

festivals.name: BrisAsia Festival
festivals.description: Brisbane's celebration of Asian culture featuring food, music, dance, arts, and cultural performances from across the Asia-Pacific region. A vibrant festival reflecting Brisbane's multicultural community.

festivals.name: Sydney Writers Festival
festivals.description: Australia's premier literary festival featuring author talks, panel discussions, workshops, and performances celebrating literature and writing. Attracts renowned writers from around the world.

festivals.name: Duntroon Horse Trials
festivals.description: One of Australia's premier equestrian events featuring dressage, cross-country, and show jumping competitions. Attracts top riders and horse enthusiasts from across the country.

festivals.name: Queen's Birthday
festivals.description: National public holiday observed in most Australian states and territories celebrating the British monarch's official birthday with various community events.

festivals.name: Dark Mofo
festivals.description: Hobart's winter arts festival celebrating art, music, theatre, and food in the darkest months with avant-garde and underground performances. A quirky, creative celebration of Tasmanian culture.

festivals.name: Darwin Festival
festivals.description: Northern Territory's major arts and culture festival celebrating Indigenous and contemporary arts, music, theatre, and community performances. Showcases the Top End's unique cultural identity.

festivals.name: Ekka (Royal Queensland Show)
festivals.description: Brisbane's iconic agricultural show featuring livestock competitions, rural displays, showbags, rides, and entertainment celebrating Queensland's farming heritage. One of Australia's largest and longest-running shows.

festivals.name: Grampians Horse Trials
festivals.description: A major equestrian event in the stunning Grampians region featuring three-day eventing competitions attracting top riders. Combines sport with beautiful mountain scenery.

festivals.name: Alice Springs Beanie Festival
festivals.description: A quirky desert festival celebrating beanies and cold-weather gear with street entertainment, markets, and community events in the Red Centre. A unique celebration born from the outback's surprising winter chill.

festivals.name: Perth Festival
festivals.description: Western Australia's major arts festival featuring theatre, music, dance, visual arts, and literature with performances in venues across Perth. A prestigious celebration of arts and culture.

festivals.name: Waroona Wildflower Festival
festivals.description: A spring celebration of Western Australia's spectacular wildflowers with guided walks, markets, and community events in the Peel region. Features native flora at peak bloom.

festivals.name: Royal Melbourne Show
festivals.description: Victoria's premier agricultural show featuring livestock competitions, rural exhibits, showbags, rides, and carnival entertainment. A major Victorian tradition spanning over 160 years.

festivals.name: Floriade
festivals.description: Canberra's spring flower festival celebrating million blooming flowers with garden displays, live entertainment, and food stalls across Commonwealth Park. A colourful celebration of spring.

festivals.name: Oktoberfest Melbourne
festivals.description: Melbourne's celebration of Bavarian culture featuring beer, traditional food, live music, and dancing in an authentic beer hall atmosphere. A lively cultural festival with German heritage.

festivals.name: Henley-on-Todd Regatta
festivals.description: A quirky annual event in Alice Springs where teams run along a dry riverbed in homemade boats and fancy dress costumes. A unique outback tradition combining sport, humor, and community spirit.

festivals.name: Austral Shepherd Festival
festivals.description: A community festival celebrating Tasmania's pastoral heritage with sheep dog trials, wool competitions, and rural displays. Showcases the island's agricultural traditions.

festivals.name: Sydney Festival
festivals.description: Sydney's summer arts festival featuring theatre, music, dance, visual arts, and comedy across indoor and outdoor venues. One of Australia's largest and most celebrated festivals.

festivals.name: Barossa Vintage Festival
festivals.description: A biennial celebration of South Australian wine culture in the Barossa Valley featuring grape crushing, wine tastings, food, and entertainment. The region's premier cultural and culinary event.

festivals.name: Festivals of Perth
festivals.description: Perth's largest community festival celebration featuring multiple simultaneous festivals with art, culture, food, and entertainment across the city. A sprawling celebration of creativity and community.

festivals.name: Melbourne Writers Festival
festivals.description: A major literary festival featuring author talks, writing workshops, and panel discussions celebrating literature and storytelling. Attracts major Australian and international authors.

festivals.name: Sculpture by the Sea
festivals.description: An outdoor sculpture exhibition stretching along the Sydney coastal walk from Bondi to Tamarama featuring contemporary artworks. A unique fusion of art and natural landscape.

festivals.name: Melbourne Cup
festivals.description: Australia's most famous horse race held at Flemington Racecourse, known as the race that stops the nation. A major sporting and social event featuring fashion, entertainment, and betting.

festivals.name: Sydney to Hobart Yacht Race
festivals.description: One of the world's great ocean yacht races departing Sydney on Boxing Day, attracting international sailing competitors. A prestigious maritime event with significant cultural significance.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrated across Australia with family gatherings, festive meals, and Christmas festivities in the Southern Hemisphere summer.

festivals.name: Boxing Day
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrated across Australia the day after Christmas, traditionally featuring sports, picnics, and family activities in the summer weather.
